name: Ria Money Transfer
description_short: Ria Money Transfer is a global money transfer service enabling customers to send and receive funds across 190+ countries through multiple channels including online, mobile app, in-person, and WhatsApp.
description_long:
Ria Money Transfer is an international remittance and money transfer service operating a distributed network of agent locations across major U.S. cities. The company facilitates cross-border and domestic money transfers with a focus on serving immigrant communities and individuals sending funds to family abroad. Their service model relies on a hybrid network of retail partner locations—including convenience stores (7-Eleven), specialty multiservice shops, check cashers, pharmacies, and hair salons—rather than proprietary branch locations.

Ria offers multiple send methods (online, mobile app, in-person, WhatsApp) and multiple receive options (bank deposit, cash pickup, digital wallet, home delivery, ATM withdrawal). They serve 40+ countries across Africa, Asia, Europe, Latin America, North America, and Oceania. The platform includes supporting tools like a currency converter and IBAN calculator. Bill payment functionality is available at many agent locations, and customers can track transfers in real-time through their app.

Ria's primary distinction is their multi-channel send capability and extensive retail agent network that extends money transfer access to underbanked populations without requiring customers to visit traditional financial institutions. Their WhatsApp integration and mobile-first approach also differentiate them in the remittance market. The distributed agent model allows geographic accessibility but creates variability in service hours and capabilities depending on individual agent operating hours.

While Ria provides genuine accessibility for international remittances, potential customers should verify exchange rates and transfer fees before sending, as these are not disclosed on the location-finder page. The reliance on third-party agents means service quality and customer experience may vary by location. The company is suitable for established international senders but lacks the transparency typical of digital-first fintech competitors.
address: 5261 N Antioch Rd, Kansas City, MO 64119

company_info.headquarters: 5261 N Antioch Rd, Kansas City, MO 64119

pros: Extensive retail agent network including 24-hour 7-Eleven locations for round-the-clock access
Multiple send methods (online, app, in-person, WhatsApp) providing flexibility for different user preferences
Multiple receive options including bank deposit, cash pickup, digital wallet, home delivery, and ATM withdrawal
Serves 40+ countries across six continents with established infrastructure in Latin America, Africa, and Asia
Real-time transfer tracking through mobile app
Integrated financial tools including currency converter and IBAN calculator for transaction planning
Bill payment functionality available at most agent locations for additional convenience
cons: No transparent fee or exchange rate disclosure on website; customers must contact agents or use app to compare costs
Service quality and hours vary significantly by location since operations depend on third-party agents
Limited customer service visibility; no clear support phone number or chat option shown on location page
Agent network includes informal multiservice shops with variable reliability and professionalism
No indication of regulatory oversight, licensing information, or complaint handling procedures on public-facing pages
best_for: International remittance senders with family in Latin America, Africa, or Asia seeking convenient pickup locations
Unbanked and underbanked immigrants in major U.S. metropolitan areas who prefer cash transactions
Customers needing multiple receive options including home delivery or digital wallet transfers
Users preferring in-person transactions over fully digital platforms for security or comfort reasons
diagnosis: Ria Money Transfer is best for immigrants and international senders who need accessible, multi-channel remittance options through a widely-distributed retail network. The main caveat is that fees, exchange rates, and service quality vary by agent location, requiring customers to shop around and verify costs before sending, and the lack of transparent pricing on the website makes cost comparison difficult.
services: In-person money transfers at retail agent locations
